task_name: End-to-Side Vascular Anastomosis using Double-Anchor Technique
explanation: This video is an educational demonstration of an advanced surgical technique (vascular anastomosis) from Baylor College of Medicine. It is NOT an FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) task. The FLS curriculum consists of five specific tasks: Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying, and Intracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying. This vascular anastomosis demonstration is a specialized surgical technique outside the FLS scope and is presented as instructional/educational content with a title slide and expert demonstration by Neal R. Barshes, M.D., M.P.H.
reason: This is an instructional video demonstrating an advanced vascular surgical technique, not an FLS task. FLS tasks are limited to the five specified exercises designed to teach fundamental laparoscopic skills. No FLS score can be assigned.

video_content_summary.source: Baylor College of Medicine, Michael E. DeBakey Department of Surgery
video_content_summary.instructor: Neal R. Barshes, M.D., M.P.H., Assistant Professor of Surgery
video_content_summary.topic: End-to-side vascular anastomosis using double-anchor technique on a Penrose drain model
techniques_demonstrated: Intracorporeal knot tying in vascular context
